rulings.Case Title: BENJAMIN J MOREHEAD et al VS. OWENS-ILLINOIS, INC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder Of Defendant Georgia-Pacific Llc, Fka Georgia-Pacific Corporation To Defendant Lowes Companies, Inc. And Lowes Home Centers, Llcs Motion To Apply Virginia Law
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, April 28, 2015 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Georgia-Pacific LLC's joinder to Lowe's Companies, Inc.'s and Lowe's Home Centers, LLC's motion to apply Virginia law is denied without prejudice to being raised within a dispositive motion or, if no such motion is filed by defendant, then before the trial judge. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: LARRY HIGHTOWER ET AL VS. ASBESTOS COMPANIES ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Defendant Alcatel Lucent Usa, Inc.'S Affirmative Defense Nos. One Through Forty Two [Code Of Civ. Proc. § 437c(F)(1)]
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, April 28, 2015 in Department 503, Line 2. Plaintiffs' motion for summary adjudication of defendant Alcatel Lucent USA, Inc.'s affirmative defenses nos. one through forty-two is granted. Plaintiffs' motion will be considered because they gave proper notice pursuant to this Court's order specially setting a trial date, which did not limit the agreed-upon summary motion briefing schedule to motions brought by defendants. Defendant's request to deny the motion, or at least to continue it, pursuant to C.C.P. 437c(h) is denied for failure to meet the requirements of section 437c(h). As those were defendant's only arguments opposing the motion, the motion is substantively unopposed and thus granted in its entirety. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: LARRY HIGHTOWER ET AL VS. ASBESTOS COMPANIES ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, April 28, 2015 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant Alcatel-Lucent USA, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs' action against it is barred by Georgia's Workers' Compensation scheme. However, even if defendant had sustained its burden, plaintiffs' Exhibits A through K would create a triable issue whether plaintiffs' action is barred by Georgia's Workers' Compensation scheme. If the Court were to get to plaintiffs' evidence, the Court would not consider Mr. Garofolo's declaration due to relevance. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: LARRY HIGHTOWER ET AL VS. ASBESTOS COMPANIES ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Requiring Plaintiffs To File An Undertaking
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, April 28, 2015 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant Alcatel-Lucent USA, Inc.'s motion for an order requiring plaintiffs to file an undertaking is denied. Defendant failed to demonstrate that there is a reasonable possibility that it will obtain judgment in the action, as required by C.C.P. § 1030. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
